proposal_text: This research proposes to deploy Amazon Rekognition, a cloud-based facial recognition system, for use by law enforcement agencies across the United States to identify individuals in real-time surveillance footage, body camera recordings, and crime scene images. The system can identify and track up to 100 faces in a single image and match them against databases of tens of millions of faces drawn from arrest records and public sources. The system will be marketed to local police departments as a tool for identifying persons of interest in criminal investigations. The default confidence threshold of 80% will be used for initial deployment, with Amazon recommending a 99% threshold for law enforcement use — a discrepancy between default settings and recommended settings that has not been addressed in the deployment contract. An ACLU audit found that at 80% confidence, Rekognition falsely matched 28 members of the US Congress to criminal mugshots, with disproportionate false positives for people of colour including six members of the Congressional Black Caucus. No independent demographic bias audit has been conducted prior to law enforcement deployment. No independent oversight mechanism or accountability framework for false match consequences has been established.

assessment.risk_summary: The proposed research involves the deployment of Amazon Rekognition, a facial recognition system, for use by law enforcement agencies to identify individuals in real-time surveillance footage. This raises concerns about bias, accuracy, and potential harm to vulnerable groups.

identified_risks.explanation: The system may perpetuate existing biases against people of color, as evidenced by the ACLU audit.
The system's 80% confidence threshold has been shown to result in inaccurate matches, including for members of Congress.
There is no independent oversight mechanism or accountability framework for false match consequences.

identified_risks.risk: Potential for mass surveillance and data collection
identified_risks.explanation: The system will be used by law enforcement agencies to identify individuals in real-time surveillance footage, raising concerns about mass surveillance and data collection.
No independent demographic bias audit has been conducted prior to law enforcement deployment.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Research must contribute to society and human well-being — discuss how applications may harm individuals, groups or society and ways to mitigate such harms
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Conduct a thorough analysis of the potential harms and benefits of the system and develop strategies to mitigate any identified risks.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Avoid harm — consider how data, code or other outcomes of research may cause harm
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Conduct a thorough risk assessment to identify potential harms and develop strategies to mitigate them.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Be fair and take action not to discriminate — consider and ameliorate biases the approach may perpetuate
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Conduct an independent demographic bias audit to identify any potential biases in the system.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Respect privacy — obtain informed consent where required and protect personally identifiable information
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Develop a plan to ensure that individuals' personal data is protected and that they are informed about how their data will be used.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: High-risk AI systems must undergo a risk assessment and mitigation plan
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Conduct a thorough risk assessment to identify potential harms and develop strategies to mitigate them.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Design and implement systems that are robustly and usably secure — especially systems close to production deployment
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Develop a plan to ensure the system is secure and that any vulnerabilities are addressed.

charter_rights_at_risk.relevance: The system may perpetuate existing biases against people of color.
The system will be used to identify individuals in real-time surveillance footage, raising concerns about mass surveillance and data collection.
The system will collect and process large amounts of personal data.

historical_precedents.incident_title: Detroit Police Facial Recognition False Arrest
historical_precedents.lesson: The importance of conducting thorough risk assessments and developing strategies to mitigate potential harms.
The need for independent demographic bias audits to identify any potential biases in the system.

recommended_mitigations.mitigation: Conduct a thorough risk assessment to identify potential harms and develop strategies to mitigate them.
Develop a plan to ensure that individuals' personal data is protected and that they are informed about how their data will be used.

tier1_mandatory_actions: Conduct an independent demographic bias audit
Develop a plan to ensure the system is secure
tier2_reflective_prompts: Consider the potential long-term consequences of deploying facial recognition technology in law enforcement agencies.
Reflect on the importance of transparency and accountability in AI development.
